title: oxalis minima
intro: Oxalis minima, also known as the small wood-sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family. It is a small, delicate perennial that has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant contains oxalic acid, which has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, inflammation, and as a natural antibacterial agent. Oxalis minima can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and it has been traditionally used to promote digestive health, reduce fever, and support the immune system.
